input.contents:
# Workspace: Skill Iteration Design

This document defines the design for `ai-pkgs workspace` — a feature that lets
users iterate on skills locally and push/pull changes to/from Git remotes.
Skills only; plugin workspace support is deferred.

---

## Command Tree

```text
ai-pkgs workspace (alias: ws)
  ├── link <name>           Link a skill for local iteration
  ├── unlink <name>         Move a workspace skill back to skills[]
  ├── push <name>           Push local changes to remote
  ├── pull <name>           Pull latest from remote into local
  ├── status [name]         Show dirty/clean state vs last push
  └── list                  List all workspace skills

ai-pkgs skills add <source> --workspace
  └── Install directly into workspace.skills (skip skills[])
```

Shorthand: `ai-pkgs ws push explain` is equivalent to
`ai-pkgs workspace push explain`.

---

## Manifest Shape

Workspace entries live in `ai-package.json` under `workspace.skills`, keyed by
skill name. A skill appears in `skills` **or** `workspace.skills`, never both.

```json
{
  "skills": {
    "tdd": {
      "source": "github:mattpocock/skills",
      "path": "skills/engineering/tdd",
      "version": "main@b843cb5"
    }
  },
  "plugins": { ... },
  "workspace": {
    "skills": {
      "explain": {
        "local": ".cursor/skills/explain",
        "source": "github:entireio/skills",
        "path": "skills/explain",
        "version": "main@c376dc9"
      },
      "my-new-skill": {
        "local": ".claude/skills/my-new-skill",
        "source": "git@code.gitlab.org:myteam/agent-skills.git",
        "path": "skills/my-new-skill",
        "version": "main@0000000"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Field Rules

| Field     | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `local`   | yes      | Relative path from project root to the skill directory on disk. |
| `source`  | yes      | Git remote. Same format as `skills[].source`: `github:owner/repo`, `gitlab:https://host/repo.git`, `git@host:owner/repo.git`. The user must have push access. |
| `path`    | yes      | Directory inside the remote repo that contains this skill. |
| `version` | yes      | `<branch>@<commitSha>`. The branch is locked at link time and cannot be changed. The commit SHA updates automatically after each push or pull. |

### Exclusivity Rule

A skill name exists in exactly one location:

- `skills` — dependency mode (consumer, read-only from user's perspective)
- `workspace.skills` — development mode (author, push/pull enabled)

`workspace link` moves a skill from `skills` → `workspace.skills`.
`workspace unlink` moves it back from `workspace.skills` → `skills`.

`skills add`, `skills update`, and `skills outdated` ignore workspace skills
entirely. Workspace skills are managed exclusively through `workspace` commands.

---

## Commands

### `ai-pkgs workspace link <name>`

Create a workspace entry for a skill. Two flows: linking an already-installed
skill, or linking a new skill from scratch.

#### Flow A: Link an existing installed skill

The skill already exists in `skills[]`. The CLI moves it to `workspace.skills`,
asking for the local path on disk.

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace link explain

┌  Workspace link
│
◇  Found "explain" in skills (github:entireio/skills)
│
◇  Local skill path?  (where the skill lives on disk)
│  .cursor/skills/explain
│
◆  Moved "explain" from skills to workspace
│
│  local:   .cursor/skills/explain
│  source:  github:entireio/skills
│  path:    skills/explain
│  version: main@c376dc9
│
└  Link complete
```

#### Flow B: Link a new skill from scratch

The skill is not in `skills[]`. The CLI asks for all fields.

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace link my-new-skill

┌  Workspace link
│
▲  "my-new-skill" not found in skills. Creating new workspace entry.
│
◇  Local skill path?
│  .claude/skills/my-new-skill
│
◇  Git remote?  (e.g. owner/repo or git@host:owner/repo.git)
│  myteam/agent-skills
│
◇  Path inside the repo?  (directory containing this skill)
│  skills/my-new-skill
│
◇  Branch?
│  main
│
◆  Created workspace entry for "my-new-skill"
│
│  local:   .claude/skills/my-new-skill
│  source:  github:myteam/agent-skills
│  path:    skills/my-new-skill
│  version: main@0000000
│
└  Link complete
```

`--ai` mode output:

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace link explain --ai

◇  Moved "explain" from skills to workspace
◇  local: .cursor/skills/explain
◇  source: github:entireio/skills
◇  path: skills/explain
◇  version: main@c376dc9
◆  Link complete
```

Flags:

- `--local <path>` — skill path on disk, skip prompt.
- `--source <remote>` — git remote, skip prompt (required for new skills in
  non-interactive mode).
- `--path <path>` — path inside the repo, skip prompt.
- `--branch <branch>` — branch to lock, skip prompt (default: `main`).

Errors:

- Skill already in `workspace.skills`: `"explain" is already a workspace skill.`
- New skill in `--ai` mode without `--source`: `--source is required when
  linking a new skill in non-interactive mode.`

### `ai-pkgs workspace unlink <name>`

Move a workspace skill back to `skills[]`, freezing it at the current version.

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace unlink explain

┌  Workspace unlink
│
◆  Moved "explain" from workspace back to skills
│
│  source:  github:entireio/skills
│  path:    skills/explain
│  version: main@c376dc9
│
└  Unlink complete
```

The `local` field is dropped. The skill becomes a regular dependency again.

Errors:

- Skill not in workspace: `"tdd" is not a workspace skill. Available: explain`

### `ai-pkgs workspace push <name>`

Push local skill changes to the remote Git repository.

#### Flow: Clean push (no conflicts)

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace push explain

┌  Workspace push
│
◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)...
│
◇  Commit message?  (default: chore: update explain)
│  feat: improve code explanation examples
│
◇  Copying .cursor/skills/explain -> skills/explain
│
◆  Committed: feat: improve code explanation examples
│
◇  Pushing to main...
│
◆  Pushed main@d4e5f6a
│
│  Updated version: main@c376dc9 -> main@d4e5f6a
│
└  Push complete
```

#### Flow: Conflict (remote has diverged)

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace push explain

┌  Workspace push
│
◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)...
│
◇  Commit message?
│  chore: update explain
│
◇  Copying .cursor/skills/explain -> skills/explain
│
◆  Committed: chore: update explain
│
◇  Pushing to main...
│
■  Push failed: remote has newer commits.
│
│  The working clone is preserved at:
│    /tmp/ai-pkgs-ws-a1b2c3/
│
│  To resolve:
│    cd /tmp/ai-pkgs-ws-a1b2c3
│    git pull --rebase origin main
│    # resolve conflicts
│    git push origin main
│
│  Then update ai-package.json:
│    ai-pkgs workspace pull explain
│
└  Push failed
```

`--ai` mode output (clean):

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace push explain --ai --message "feat: improve examples"

◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)
◇  Copying .cursor/skills/explain -> skills/explain
◇  Committed: feat: improve examples
◇  Pushing to main
◆  Pushed main@d4e5f6a
◆  Push complete
```

Flags:

- `--message <msg>` / `-m <msg>` — commit message, skip prompt.
- `--ai` — non-interactive: auto-generates commit message if `--message` not
  provided (`chore: update <skill-name>`).

Errors:

- Skill not in workspace: `"tdd" is not a workspace skill.`
- Local path does not exist: `Local path ".cursor/skills/explain" does not
  exist. Did you move or delete the skill?`
- Authentication failure: `Push failed: authentication error. Check your Git
  credentials for github:entireio/skills.`

### `ai-pkgs workspace pull <name>`

Pull the latest version of a skill from the remote into the local directory.

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace pull explain

┌  Workspace pull
│
◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)...
│
◆  Pulled skills/explain -> .cursor/skills/explain
│
│  Updated version: main@c376dc9 -> main@f7890ab
│
└  Pull complete
```

#### Flow: Local has uncommitted changes

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace pull explain

┌  Workspace pull
│
◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)...
│
▲  Local directory .cursor/skills/explain has changes since last push.
│  Pulling will overwrite them.
│
◇  Continue?
│  ● Yes, overwrite local changes
│  ○ No, cancel
│
◆  Pulled skills/explain -> .cursor/skills/explain
│
│  Updated version: main@c376dc9 -> main@f7890ab
│
└  Pull complete
```

`--ai` mode: pull always overwrites without prompting. Use `workspace status`
first to check for local changes.

Flags:

- `--force` — skip the overwrite confirmation.

Errors:

- Skill not in workspace: `"tdd" is not a workspace skill.`
- Remote fetch failed: `Failed to fetch github:entireio/skills. Check the
  remote URL and your network connection.`

### `ai-pkgs workspace status [name]`

Show the sync state of workspace skills. Compares local files against the last
pushed/pulled commit.

#### Single skill

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace status explain

┌  Workspace status
│
◇  explain ─────────────────────────────────╮
│                                            │
│  local:   .cursor/skills/explain           │
│  source:  github:entireio/skills           │
│  path:    skills/explain                   │
│  version: main@c376dc9                     │
│  status:  modified (local has changes)     │
│                                            │
├────────────────────────────────────────────╯
│
└  Done.
```

#### All skills

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace status

┌  Workspace status
│
◇  Workspace skills (2) ────────────────────╮
│                                            │
│  explain                                   │
│    status: modified (local has changes)    │
│    version: main@c376dc9                   │
│                                            │
│  my-new-skill                              │
│    status: clean                           │
│    version: main@a1b2c3d                   │
│                                            │
├────────────────────────────────────────────╯
│
└  Done.
```

Status values:

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `clean` | Local matches last pushed version |
| `modified` | Local has changes since last push/pull |
| `untracked` | Version is `0000000` (never pushed) |

Status detection: clone the remote at the pinned `commitSha`, extract the skill
directory at `path`, and diff against the `local` directory. If any files differ
in content or are added/removed, the status is `modified`.

Plain-text output (non-TTY / `--ai`):

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace status --ai

explain: modified main@c376dc9
my-new-skill: clean main@a1b2c3d
```

### `ai-pkgs workspace list`

List all workspace skill entries.

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace list

┌  Workspace skills
│
◇  Workspace skills (2) ────────────────────────╮
│                                                │
│  explain                                       │
│    local:   .cursor/skills/explain             │
│    source:  github:entireio/skills             │
│    path:    skills/explain                     │
│    version: main@c376dc9                       │
│                                                │
│  my-new-skill                                  │
│    local:   .claude/skills/my-new-skill        │
│    source:  github:myteam/agent-skills         │
│    path:    skills/my-new-skill                │
│    version: main@0000000                       │
│                                                │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
│
└  Done.
```

Empty state:

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace list

┌  Workspace skills
│
▲  No workspace skills. Run `ai-pkgs workspace link <name>` to get started.
│
└  Done.
```

Flags:

- `--json` — machine-readable output.
- `--ai` — plain-text output.

JSON output:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "explain",
    "local": ".cursor/skills/explain",
    "source": "github:entireio/skills",
    "path": "skills/explain",
    "version": "main@c376dc9"
  }
]
```

### `ai-pkgs skills add <source> --workspace`

Install a skill directly into `workspace.skills`, skipping `skills[]`.

```
$ ai-pkgs skills add entireio/skills --skill explain --workspace

┌  Skill add (workspace)
│
◇  Repository cloned (main@c376dc9)
│
◇  Discovered 5 skill(s)
│
◇  Local skill path?  (where to place the skill on disk)
│  .cursor/skills/explain
│
◇  Installing skill...
│
│  copy: explain -> .cursor/skills/explain
│
◆  Wrote workspace entry for "explain"
│
│  local:   .cursor/skills/explain
│  source:  github:entireio/skills
│  path:    skills/explain
│  version: main@c376dc9
│
└  Add complete
```

This combines `skills add` resolution + discovery with `workspace link`
manifest writing. The skill is installed to the `local` path (not to agent
target directories), and only the workspace entry is written.

Flags:

All standard `skills add` flags apply, plus:

- `--workspace` — route the entry to `workspace.skills` instead of `skills[]`.
  When set, the CLI prompts for a `local` path and skips the agent target
  selector.

Errors:

- `--workspace` with `--global`: `--workspace and --global are mutually
  exclusive. Workspace skills are always project-scoped.`

---

## Design Rules

### Branch Locking

The `version` field encodes both the branch name and the commit SHA:
`main@c376dc9`. The branch is set at `workspace link` time and cannot be
changed afterward. All push and pull operations target this locked branch.

To switch branches, `unlink` and re-`link` with a new `--branch` value.

### No Fork / Upstream Tracking

`source` is both where the skill was originally obtained AND where changes are
pushed to. There is no separate `upstream` field.

If you want to push to a different repo than where you installed from (fork
workflow), create the target repo yourself, then use `workspace link` with that
repo as the `source`.

### Commit Message

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| TTY  | Prompt with default `chore: update <skill-name>`. User can type a custom message. |
| `--message` flag | Use the provided message, skip prompt. |
| `--ai` mode | Auto-generate `chore: update <skill-name>` unless `--message` is provided. |

### Conflict Resolution

`workspace push` performs a straightforward sequence:

1. Clone/fetch the remote repo (reuse Git cache where possible).
2. Checkout the locked branch.
3. Copy the local skill directory into the clone at `path` (full directory
   overwrite).
4. `git add . && git commit -m "<message>"`.
5. `git push origin <branch>`.
6. If push succeeds: update `version` in `ai-package.json`.
7. If push fails (remote diverged): **bail out**. Print the temp clone path and
   manual resolution instructions. Do not attempt auto-rebase.

The user resolves conflicts manually in the temp clone directory using standard
Git tools, then runs `workspace pull` to sync the resolved state back to their
local directory.

### Pull Overwrite Warning

`workspace pull` replaces the local skill directory with the remote version. If
the local directory has changes since the last push/pull (detected by diffing
against the pinned commit), the CLI warns and asks for confirmation in TTY mode.

In `--ai` mode or with `--force`, pull always overwrites without prompting.

---

## Push / Pull Data Flow

```text
workspace push explain
  │
  ├── 1. Read workspace entry from ai-package.json
  │      { local: ".cursor/skills/explain", source: "github:entireio/skills",
  │        path: "skills/explain", version: "main@c376dc9" }
  │
  ├── 2. Clone/fetch entireio/skills into temp dir
  │      /tmp/ai-pkgs-ws-xxxxx/
  │
  ├── 3. Checkout branch "main"
  │
  ├── 4. Copy .cursor/skills/explain/ -> /tmp/.../skills/explain/
  │      (full directory replace)
  │
  ├── 5. git add . && git commit -m "chore: update explain"
  │
  ├── 6. git push origin main
  │      ├── success: update version to main@<new-sha>
  │      └── failure: print temp path, bail
  │
  └── 7. Clean up temp dir (on success only)

workspace pull explain
  │
  ├── 1. Read workspace entry from ai-package.json
  │
  ├── 2. Clone/fetch entireio/skills at latest
  │
  ├── 3. Copy /tmp/.../skills/explain/ -> .cursor/skills/explain/
  │      (full directory replace, warn if local has changes)
  │
  ├── 4. Get latest commit SHA on branch
  │
  └── 5. Update version to main@<latest-sha>
```

---

## CLI Architecture

```text
src/
  commands/
    workspace/
      index.ts          # registerWorkspaceCommand, dispatch (alias: ws)
      link.ts           # workspace link
      unlink.ts         # workspace unlink
      push.ts           # workspace push
      pull.ts           # workspace pull
      status.ts         # workspace status
      list.ts           # workspace list
      types.ts          # WorkspaceCommandOptions, WorkspaceSkillEntry
  manifest/
    parse.ts            # extend to parse workspace.skills
    store.ts            # extend with addWorkspaceSkill, removeWorkspaceSkill,
                        #   moveSkillToWorkspace, moveWorkspaceToSkill
    types.ts            # extend RawAiPackageManifest with workspace
  types.ts              # extend AiPackageManifest with workspace
```

### Manifest Store Extensions

New methods on the manifest store:

- `addWorkspaceSkill(entry)` — add or update a workspace skill entry.
- `removeWorkspaceSkill(name)` — remove a workspace skill entry.
- `moveSkillToWorkspace(name, workspaceFields)` — atomically move a skill from
  `skills` to `workspace.skills`.
- `moveWorkspaceToSkill(name)` — atomically move a workspace skill back to
  `skills`.

### Source Parsing

The `source` field in workspace entries reuses the existing source format parser
from `skills[].source`. The `github:`, `gitlab:`, and raw Git URL formats are
all supported. The registry resolution logic from `skills add` is reused for
clone/fetch operations during push and pull.

---

## Interaction with Existing Commands

| Command | Behavior with workspace skills |
|---------|-------------------------------|
| `skills add` | Ignores workspace skills. `--workspace` flag routes to `workspace.skills`. |
| `skills list` | Does not show workspace skills. |
| `skills update` | Does not update workspace skills. |
| `skills outdated` | Does not check workspace skills. |
| `skills remove` | Cannot remove a workspace skill. Prints: `"explain" is a workspace skill. Use workspace unlink first.` |
| `ai-pkgs install` | Does not install workspace skills (they are already at their `local` path). |
| `workspace list` | Shows only workspace skills. |
| `workspace push/pull` | Only operates on workspace skills. |

---

## Deferred Work

- **Plugin workspace**: `workspace.plugins` with push/pull for plugin
  directories. Deferred because plugins have vendor dirs, marketplace manifests,
  and multi-target installation that add complexity.
- **`workspace diff`**: Show a file-level diff between local and last-pushed
  version.
- **`workspace sync`**: Bidirectional sync (pull then push) in a single command.
- **PR creation**: `workspace push --pr` to open a pull request instead of
  pushing directly to the branch.
- **Multi-skill push**: `workspace push --all` to push all modified workspace
  skills at once.
- **Branch switching**: `workspace branch <name> <new-branch>` to change the
  locked branch (currently requires unlink + re-link).

## Sources
input.new_string:
- `--provider <provider>`: filter by `github` or `gitlab`.
- `--source <source>`: filter by source, such as `owner/repo` or a clone URL.

### `ai-pkgs workspace link <name> [options]`

Create a workspace entry for a skill. If the skill is in `skills[]`, moves it
to `workspace.skills`. Otherwise creates a new entry (requires `--source` in
non-interactive mode).

Flags:

- `--local <path>`: local skill path on disk.
- `--source <remote>`: Git remote URL or shorthand.
- `--path <path>`: path inside the remote repo.
- `--branch <branch>`: branch to lock (default: `main`).

### `ai-pkgs workspace unlink <name> [options]`

Removes the workspace entry and deletes the local skill directory from disk.
Requires confirmation in TTY; requires `--yes` in `--ai` mode.

Flags:

- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompt.

### `ai-pkgs workspace push <name> [options]`

Pushes local skill changes to the remote Git repo. Copies the local directory
into a clone of the remote at the configured path, commits, and pushes. On
conflict (remote has diverged), bails out and prints the temp clone path for
manual resolution.

Flags:

- `-m, --message <msg>`: commit message (default: `chore: update <name>`).
- `-y, --yes`: skip commit message prompt.

### `ai-pkgs workspace pull <name> [options]`

Pulls the latest version from the remote into the local directory. Warns if
local has changes since last push.

Flags:

- `--force`: skip overwrite confirmation.

### `ai-pkgs workspace status [name]`

Shows sync state of workspace skills: `clean`, `modified`, or `untracked`.

### `ai-pkgs workspace list [options]`

Lists all workspace skill entries.

Flags:

- `--json`: print machine-readable output.

## Sources

input.contents:
# Workspace: Skill Iteration Design

This document defines the design for `ai-pkgs workspace` — a feature that lets
users iterate on skills locally and push/pull changes to/from Git remotes.
Skills only; plugin workspace support is deferred.

---

## Command Tree

```text
ai-pkgs workspace (alias: ws)
  ├── link <name>           Move an installed skill to workspace for iteration
  ├── remove <name>         Remove workspace entry and delete local skill files
  ├── push <name>           Push local changes to remote
  ├── pull <name>           Pull latest from remote into local
  ├── status [name]         Show dirty/clean state vs last push
  └── list                  List all workspace skills

ai-pkgs skills add <source> --workspace
  └── Install directly into workspace.skills (skip skills[])
```

Shorthand: `ai-pkgs ws push explain` is equivalent to
`ai-pkgs workspace push explain`.

---

## Manifest Shape

Workspace entries live in `ai-package.json` under `workspace.skills`, keyed by
skill name. A skill appears in `skills` **or** `workspace.skills`, never both.

```json
{
  "skills": {
    "tdd": {
      "source": "github:mattpocock/skills",
      "path": "skills/engineering/tdd",
      "version": "main@b843cb5"
    }
  },
  "plugins": { ... },
  "workspace": {
    "skills": {
      "explain": {
        "local": ".cursor/skills/explain",
        "source": "github:entireio/skills",
        "path": "skills/explain",
        "version": "main@c376dc9"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Field Rules

| Field     | Required | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `local`   | yes      | Relative path from project root to the skill directory on disk. |
| `source`  | yes      | Git remote. Same format as `skills[].source`: `github:owner/repo`, `gitlab:https://host/repo.git`, `git@host:owner/repo.git`, `https://github.com/owner/repo`. The user must have push access. |
| `path`    | yes      | Directory inside the remote repo that contains this skill. |
| `version` | yes      | `<branch>@<commitSha>`. The branch is locked at link time and cannot be changed. The commit SHA updates automatically after each push or pull. |

### Exclusivity Rule

A skill name exists in exactly one location:

- `skills` — dependency mode (consumer, read-only from user's perspective)
- `workspace.skills` — development mode (author, push/pull enabled)

`workspace link` moves a skill from `skills` → `workspace.skills`. It only
works for skills already present in `skills[]`.

`workspace remove` removes the workspace entry and deletes the local skill
directory from disk. The skill is fully removed — if you want it back as a
dependency afterward, run `skills add` separately.

For new skills that are not yet in `skills[]`, use
`skills add owner/repo --workspace` to install directly into workspace.

`skills add`, `skills update`, and `skills outdated` ignore workspace skills
entirely. Workspace skills are managed exclusively through `workspace` commands.

---

## Commands

### `ai-pkgs workspace link <name>`

Move an already-installed skill from `skills[]` to `workspace.skills` for local
iteration. The `source`, `path`, and `version` are auto-filled from the existing
manifest entry. The only prompt is an agent-aware local path selector.

`workspace link` only works for skills already in `skills[]`. For new skills,
use `skills add --workspace` instead.

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace link explain

┌  Workspace link
│
◇  Found "explain" in skills (github:entireio/skills)
│
◇  Which installed copy to iterate on?
│  ● .cursor/skills/explain
│  ○ .claude/skills/explain
│  ○ Custom path (type manually)
│
◆  Moved "explain" to workspace
│
│  local:   .cursor/skills/explain
│  source:  github:entireio/skills
│  path:    skills/explain
│  version: main@c376dc9
│
└  Link complete
```

The selector scans agent skill directories (using the agent registry) for the
skill folder. Found paths are listed first, followed by a "Custom path" option
that opens a text input. If the selected or typed path does not exist on disk,
the CLI shows an error and re-prompts.

#### Custom path with validation

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace link explain

┌  Workspace link
│
◇  Found "explain" in skills (github:entireio/skills)
│
◇  Which installed copy to iterate on?
│  ○ .cursor/skills/explain
│  ○ .claude/skills/explain
│  ● Custom path (type manually)
│
◇  Enter path:
│  .nonexistent/skills/explain
│
■  Path does not exist: .nonexistent/skills/explain
│
◇  Enter path:
│  .cursor/skills/explain
│
◆  Moved "explain" to workspace
│
│  local:   .cursor/skills/explain
│  source:  github:entireio/skills
│  path:    skills/explain
│  version: main@c376dc9
│
└  Link complete
```

`--ai` mode output:

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace link explain --ai --local .cursor/skills/explain

◇  Found "explain" in skills (github:entireio/skills)
◆  Moved "explain" to workspace
◇  local: .cursor/skills/explain
◇  source: github:entireio/skills
◇  path: skills/explain
◇  version: main@c376dc9
◆  Link complete
```

Flags:

- `--local <path>` — local skill path on disk, skip selector prompt. Required
  in `--ai` mode.

Errors:

- Skill not in `skills[]`: `"my-new-skill" is not in skills. Use
  "skills add <source> --workspace" to add a new workspace skill.`
- Skill already in workspace: `"explain" is already a workspace skill.`
- `--ai` mode without `--local`: `--local is required when linking in
  non-interactive mode.`
- `--local` path does not exist: `Path does not exist:
  .nonexistent/skills/explain`

### `ai-pkgs workspace remove <name>`

Remove a workspace skill entry from `ai-package.json` and delete the local
skill directory from disk. This is a destructive operation — the skill files
are permanently removed.

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace remove explain

┌  Workspace remove
│
▲  This will remove the workspace entry and delete local files:
│    .cursor/skills/explain
│
◇  Delete .cursor/skills/explain and remove workspace entry?
│  ● Yes, delete and remove
│  ○ No, cancel
│
◆  Deleted .cursor/skills/explain
◆  Removed "explain" from workspace
│
└  Remove complete
```

`--ai` mode requires `--yes`:

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace remove explain --ai --yes

◇  Deleting .cursor/skills/explain
◆  Deleted .cursor/skills/explain
◆  Removed "explain" from workspace
◆  Remove complete
```

Without `--yes` in `--ai` mode:

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace remove explain --ai

■  Pass --yes to confirm removal in non-interactive mode.
```

Flags:

- `-y, --yes` — skip confirmation prompt (required in `--ai` mode).

Errors:

- Skill not in workspace: `"tdd" is not a workspace skill. Available: explain`
- Local path does not exist: proceeds anyway (removes the workspace entry and
  warns that the directory was already missing).

### `ai-pkgs workspace push <name>`

Push local skill changes to the remote Git repository.

#### Flow: Clean push (no conflicts)

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace push explain

┌  Workspace push
│
◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)...
│
◇  Commit message?  (default: chore: update explain)
│  feat: improve code explanation examples
│
◇  Copying .cursor/skills/explain -> skills/explain
│
◆  Committed: feat: improve code explanation examples
│
◇  Pushing to main...
│
◆  Pushed main@d4e5f6a
│
│  Updated version: main@c376dc9 -> main@d4e5f6a
│
└  Push complete
```

#### Flow: Conflict — TTY interactive resolution

When the remote has newer commits and push is rejected, TTY mode shows a
selector letting the user choose how to resolve:

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace push explain

┌  Workspace push
│
◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)...
│
◇  Commit message?
│  chore: update explain
│
◇  Copying .cursor/skills/explain -> skills/explain
│
◆  Committed: chore: update explain
│
◇  Pushing to main...
│
▲  Push failed: remote has newer commits.
│
◇  How to resolve?
│  ● Accept my changes (force push, overwrite remote)
│  ○ Resolve locally (keep temp clone for manual merge)
│
◇  Force pushing to main...
│
◆  Pushed main@d4e5f6a (force)
│
│  Updated version: main@c376dc9 -> main@d4e5f6a
│
└  Push complete
```

If the user selects "Resolve locally":

```
◇  How to resolve?
│  ○ Accept my changes (force push, overwrite remote)
│  ● Resolve locally (keep temp clone for manual merge)
│
│  The working clone is preserved at:
│    /tmp/ai-pkgs-ws-a1b2c3/
│
│  To resolve:
│    cd /tmp/ai-pkgs-ws-a1b2c3
│    git pull --rebase origin main
│    # resolve conflicts
│    git push origin main
│
│  Then sync your local copy:
│    ai-pkgs workspace pull explain
│
└  Push failed (manual resolution required)
```

#### Flow: Conflict — `--accept-my-change` flag

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace push explain --accept-my-change -m "chore: update explain"

┌  Workspace push
│
◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)...
│
◇  Copying .cursor/skills/explain -> skills/explain
│
◆  Committed: chore: update explain
│
◇  Pushing to main...
│
▲  Push rejected, force pushing with --accept-my-change...
│
◆  Pushed main@d4e5f6a (force)
│
│  Updated version: main@c376dc9 -> main@d4e5f6a
│
└  Push complete
```

#### Flow: Conflict — `--ai` mode without `--accept-my-change` (safe default)

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace push explain --ai -m "chore: update explain"

◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)
◇  Copying .cursor/skills/explain -> skills/explain
◇  Committed: chore: update explain
◇  Pushing to main
■  Push failed: remote has newer commits.
■  The working clone is preserved at: /tmp/ai-pkgs-ws-a1b2c3/
■  Pass --accept-my-change to force push, or resolve manually.
```

`--ai` mode output (clean push):

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace push explain --ai -m "feat: improve examples"

◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)
◇  Copying .cursor/skills/explain -> skills/explain
◇  Committed: feat: improve examples
◇  Pushing to main
◆  Pushed main@d4e5f6a
◆  Push complete
```

Conflict resolution behavior matrix:

| Mode | No flag | `--accept-my-change` |
|------|---------|---------------------|
| TTY  | Show selector (accept vs resolve locally) | Force push, no prompt |
| `--ai` | Fail with bail-out message | Force push, no prompt |

Flags:

- `--message <msg>` / `-m <msg>` — commit message, skip prompt.
- `--accept-my-change` — force push when remote has diverged, overwriting
  remote with local changes.
- `--ai` — non-interactive: auto-generates commit message if `--message` not
  provided (`chore: update <skill-name>`).

Errors:

- Skill not in workspace: `"tdd" is not a workspace skill.`
- Local path does not exist: `Local path ".cursor/skills/explain" does not
  exist. Did you move or delete the skill?`
- Authentication failure: `Push failed: authentication error. Check your Git
  credentials for github:entireio/skills.`

### `ai-pkgs workspace pull <name>`

Pull the latest version of a skill from the remote into the local directory.

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace pull explain

┌  Workspace pull
│
◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)...
│
◆  Pulled skills/explain -> .cursor/skills/explain
│
│  Updated version: main@c376dc9 -> main@f7890ab
│
└  Pull complete
```

#### Flow: Local has uncommitted changes

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace pull explain

┌  Workspace pull
│
◇  Fetching github:entireio/skills (main)...
│
▲  Local directory .cursor/skills/explain has changes since last push.
│  Pulling will overwrite them.
│
◇  Continue?
│  ● Yes, overwrite local changes
│  ○ No, cancel
│
◆  Pulled skills/explain -> .cursor/skills/explain
│
│  Updated version: main@c376dc9 -> main@f7890ab
│
└  Pull complete
```

`--ai` mode: pull always overwrites without prompting. Use `workspace status`
first to check for local changes.

Flags:

- `--force` — skip the overwrite confirmation.

Errors:

- Skill not in workspace: `"tdd" is not a workspace skill.`
- Remote fetch failed: `Failed to fetch github:entireio/skills. Check the
  remote URL and your network connection.`

### `ai-pkgs workspace status [name]`

Show the sync state of workspace skills. Compares local files against the last
pushed/pulled commit.

#### Single skill

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace status explain

┌  Workspace status
│
◇  explain ─────────────────────────────────╮
│                                            │
│  local:   .cursor/skills/explain           │
│  source:  github:entireio/skills           │
│  path:    skills/explain                   │
│  version: main@c376dc9                     │
│  status:  modified (local has changes)     │
│                                            │
├────────────────────────────────────────────╯
│
└  Done.
```

#### All skills

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace status

┌  Workspace status
│
◇  Workspace skills (2) ────────────────────╮
│                                            │
│  explain                                   │
│    status: modified (local has changes)    │
│    version: main@c376dc9                   │
│                                            │
│  my-new-skill                              │
│    status: clean                           │
│    version: main@a1b2c3d                   │
│                                            │
├────────────────────────────────────────────╯
│
└  Done.
```

Status values:

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `clean` | Local matches last pushed version |
| `modified` | Local has changes since last push/pull |
| `untracked` | Version is `0000000` (never pushed) |

Status detection: clone the remote at the pinned `commitSha`, extract the skill
directory at `path`, and diff against the `local` directory. If any files differ
in content or are added/removed, the status is `modified`.

Plain-text output (non-TTY / `--ai`):

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace status --ai

explain: modified main@c376dc9
my-new-skill: clean main@a1b2c3d
```

### `ai-pkgs workspace list`

List all workspace skill entries.

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace list

┌  Workspace skills
│
◇  Workspace skills (2) ────────────────────────╮
│                                                │
│  explain                                       │
│    local:   .cursor/skills/explain             │
│    source:  github:entireio/skills             │
│    path:    skills/explain                     │
│    version: main@c376dc9                       │
│                                                │
│  my-new-skill                                  │
│    local:   .claude/skills/my-new-skill        │
│    source:  github:myteam/agent-skills         │
│    path:    skills/my-new-skill                │
│    version: main@0000000                       │
│                                                │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
│
└  Done.
```

Empty state:

```
$ ai-pkgs workspace list

┌  Workspace skills
│
▲  No workspace skills.
│  Use "ai-pkgs workspace link <name>" to iterate on an installed skill,
│  or "ai-pkgs skills add <source> --workspace" to add a new one.
│
└  Done.
```

Flags:

- `--json` — machine-readable output.
- `--ai` — plain-text output.

JSON output:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "explain",
    "local": ".cursor/skills/explain",
    "source": "github:entireio/skills",
    "path": "skills/explain",
    "version": "main@c376dc9"
  }
]
```

### `ai-pkgs skills add <source> --workspace`

Install a skill directly into `workspace.skills`, skipping `skills[]`. Use this
for new skills that are not yet in the manifest.

```
$ ai-pkgs skills add entireio/skills --skill explain --workspace

┌  Skill add (workspace)
│
◇  Repository cloned (main@c376dc9)
│
◇  Discovered 5 skill(s)
│
◇  Local skill path?  (where to place the skill on disk)
│  .cursor/skills/explain
│
◇  Installing skill...
│
│  copy: explain -> .cursor/skills/explain
│
◆  Wrote workspace entry for "explain"
│
│  local:   .cursor/skills/explain
│  source:  github:entireio/skills
│  path:    skills/explain
│  version: main@c376dc9
│
└  Add complete
```

This combines `skills add` resolution + discovery with workspace entry writing.
The skill is installed to the `local` path (not to agent target directories),
and only the workspace entry is written.

Flags:

All standard `skills add` flags apply, plus:

- `--workspace` — route the entry to `workspace.skills` instead of `skills[]`.
  When set, the CLI prompts for a `local` path and skips the agent target
  selector.

Errors:

- `--workspace` with `--global`: `--workspace and --global are mutually
  exclusive. Workspace skills are always project-scoped.`

---

## Design Rules

### Branch Locking

The `version` field encodes both the branch name and the commit SHA:
`main@c376dc9`. The branch is set at `workspace link` time (or `skills add
--workspace` time) and cannot be changed afterward. All push and pull operations
target this locked branch.

To switch branches, `remove` the workspace skill and re-add it with a new
branch via `skills add --workspace --ref <new-branch>`.

### No Fork / Upstream Tracking

`source` is both where the skill was originally obtained AND where changes are
pushed to. There is no separate `upstream` field.

If you want to push to a different repo than where you installed from (fork
workflow), create the target repo yourself, then use `skills add --workspace`
with that repo as the source.

### Commit Message

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| TTY  | Prompt with default `chore: update <skill-name>`. User can type a custom message. |
| `--message` flag | Use the provided message, skip prompt. |
| `--ai` mode | Auto-generate `chore: update <skill-name>` unless `--message` is provided. |

### Conflict Resolution

`workspace push` performs a straightforward sequence:

1. Clone/fetch the remote repo (reuse Git cache where possible).
2. Checkout the locked branch.
3. Copy the local skill directory into the clone at `path` (full directory
   overwrite).
4. `git add . && git commit -m "<message>"`.
5. `git push origin <branch>`.

On success: update `version` in `ai-package.json` and clean up the temp clone.

On push rejection (remote has diverged):

| Mode | No flag | `--accept-my-change` |
|------|---------|---------------------|
| TTY  | Show selector: "Accept my changes (force push)" vs "Resolve locally (manual merge)" | Force push without prompt |
| `--ai` | Fail with bail-out message and temp clone path | Force push without prompt |

"Accept my changes" performs `git push --force-with-lease origin <branch>`,
overwriting remote with the local version.

"Resolve locally" preserves the temp clone at `/tmp/ai-pkgs-ws-xxxxx/` and
prints manual resolution instructions. The user runs `workspace pull` after
resolving to sync the result back to their local directory.

### Pull Overwrite Warning

`workspace pull` replaces the local skill directory with the remote version. If
the local directory has changes since the last push/pull (detected by diffing
against the pinned commit), the CLI warns and asks for confirmation in TTY mode.

In `--ai` mode or with `--force`, pull always overwrites without prompting.

---

## Push / Pull Data Flow

```text
workspace push explain
  │
  ├── 1. Read workspace entry from ai-package.json
  │      { local: ".cursor/skills/explain", source: "github:entireio/skills",
  │        path: "skills/explain", version: "main@c376dc9" }
  │
  ├── 2. Clone/fetch entireio/skills into temp dir
  │      /tmp/ai-pkgs-ws-xxxxx/
  │
  ├── 3. Checkout branch "main"
  │
  ├── 4. Copy .cursor/skills/explain/ -> /tmp/.../skills/explain/
  │      (full directory replace)
  │
  ├── 5. git add . && git commit -m "chore: update explain"
  │
  ├── 6. git push origin main
  │      ├── success: update version to main@<new-sha>, clean up temp
  │      ├── rejected + TTY: show selector (force push vs resolve locally)
  │      ├── rejected + --accept-my-change: git push --force-with-lease
  │      └── rejected + --ai (no flag): fail, print temp path
  │
  └── 7. Clean up temp dir (on success / force push only)

workspace pull explain
  │
  ├── 1. Read workspace entry from ai-package.json
  │
  ├── 2. Clone/fetch entireio/skills at latest
  │
  ├── 3. Detect local changes (diff local vs pinned commit)
  │      ├── changes found + TTY: warn and prompt
  │      ├── changes found + --force / --ai: proceed
  │      └── no changes: proceed
  │
  ├── 4. Copy /tmp/.../skills/explain/ -> .cursor/skills/explain/
  │      (full directory replace)
  │
  ├── 5. Get latest commit SHA on branch
  │
  └── 6. Update version to main@<latest-sha>
```

---

## CLI Architecture

```text
src/
  commands/
    workspace/
      index.ts          # registerWorkspaceCommand, dispatch (alias: ws)
      link.ts           # workspace link (existing skills[] only)
      remove.ts         # workspace remove (delete entry + local files)
      push.ts           # workspace push (with --accept-my-change)
      pull.ts           # workspace pull
      status.ts         # workspace status
      list.ts           # workspace list
      types.ts          # WorkspaceCommandOptions, WorkspaceSkillEntry
  manifest/
    parse.ts            # extend to parse workspace.skills
    store.ts            # extend with addWorkspaceSkill, removeWorkspaceSkill,
                        #   moveSkillToWorkspace
    types.ts            # extend RawAiPackageManifest with workspace
  types.ts              # extend AiPackageManifest with workspace
```

### Manifest Store Extensions

New methods on the manifest store:

- `addWorkspaceSkill(entry)` — add or update a workspace skill entry.
- `removeWorkspaceSkill(name)` — remove a workspace skill entry.
- `moveSkillToWorkspace(name, localPath)` — atomically move a skill from
  `skills` to `workspace.skills`, preserving `source`, `path`, `version` and
  adding the `local` field.

### Source Parsing

The `source` field in workspace entries reuses the existing source format parser
from `skills[].source`. All formats are supported:

- `github:owner/repo`
- `gitlab:https://host/repo.git`
- `https://github.com/owner/repo`
- `git@host:owner/repo.git`

The registry resolution logic from `skills add` is reused for clone/fetch
operations during push and pull.

---

## Interaction with Existing Commands

| Command | Behavior with workspace skills |
|---------|-------------------------------|
| `skills add` | Ignores workspace skills. `--workspace` flag routes to `workspace.skills`. |
| `skills list` | Does not show workspace skills. |
| `skills update` | Does not update workspace skills. |
| `skills outdated` | Does not check workspace skills. |
| `skills remove` | Cannot remove a workspace skill. Prints: `"explain" is a workspace skill. Use workspace remove to delete it.` |
| `ai-pkgs install` | Does not install workspace skills (they are already at their `local` path). |
| `workspace list` | Shows only workspace skills. |
| `workspace push/pull` | Only operates on workspace skills. |

---

## Deferred Work

- **Plugin workspace**: `workspace.plugins` with push/pull for plugin
  directories. Deferred because plugins have vendor dirs, marketplace manifests,
  and multi-target installation that add complexity.
- **`workspace diff`**: Show a file-level diff between local and last-pushed
  version.
- **`workspace sync`**: Bidirectional sync (pull then push) in a single command.
- **PR creation**: `workspace push --pr` to open a pull request instead of
  pushing directly to the branch.
- **Multi-skill push**: `workspace push --all` to push all modified workspace
  skills at once.
- **Branch switching**: `workspace branch <name> <new-branch>` to change the
  locked branch (currently requires remove + re-add via `skills add --workspace`).

### `ai-pkgs workspace link <name> [options]`

Create a workspace entry for a skill. If the skill is in `skills[]`, moves it
to `workspace.skills`. Otherwise creates a new entry (requires `--source` in
non-interactive mode).

Flags:

- `--source <remote>`: Git remote URL or shorthand.
- `--path <path>`: path inside the remote repo.
- `--branch <branch>`: branch to lock (default: `main`).

Removes the workspace entry and deletes the local skill directory from disk.

### `ai-pkgs workspace push <name> [options]`

Pushes local skill changes to the remote Git repo. Copies the local directory
into a clone of the remote at the configured path, commits, and pushes. On
conflict (remote has diverged), bails out and prints the temp clone path for
manual resolution.

Flags:

- `-m, --message <msg>`: commit message (default: `chore: update <name>`).
- `-y, --yes`: skip commit message prompt.

### `ai-pkgs workspace pull <name> [options]`

Pulls the latest version from the remote into the local directory. Warns if
local has changes since last push.

Flags:

### `ai-pkgs workspace status [name]`

Shows sync state of workspace skills: `clean`, `modified`, or `untracked`.

Lists all workspace skill entries.

Flags:

- `--json`: print machine-readable output.

Move an installed skill from `skills[]` to `workspace.skills` for local
iteration. Only works for skills already in the manifest. Shows an agent-aware

### `ai-pkgs workspace remove <name> [options]`

Removes the workspace entry and deletes the local skill directory from disk.

### `ai-pkgs workspace push <name> [options]`

Pushes local skill changes to the remote Git repo. Copies the local directory
into a clone of the remote at the configured path, commits, and pushes. On
conflict (remote has diverged): in TTY shows a selector to accept your changes
(force push) or resolve locally (manual merge); in `--ai` mode fails by
default, or force-pushes with `--accept-my-change`.

Flags:

Lists all workspace skill entries.

content.text:
All done. Here's the summary of changes from the grilling session:

**Design doc** (`docs/ai-package-workspace.md`) -- full rewrite:
- **`link`** simplified: only works for skills already in `skills[]`. Auto-fills `source`/`path`/`version` from the manifest. Shows an agent-aware path selector (scans installed agent dirs) with a "Custom path" fallback. Validates path exists on disk, re-prompts on error. `--local` flag required in `--ai` mode.
- **`unlink` renamed to `remove`**: removes workspace entry + deletes local files. TTY confirmation, `--yes` required in `--ai` mode.
- **`push` conflict resolution**: new `--accept-my-change` flag for force-push. TTY shows a selector ("Accept my changes" vs "Resolve locally"). `--ai` mode fails safely by default, force-pushes with the flag. Full behavior matrix documented.
- Removed "Flow B: link a new skill from scratch" -- use `skills add --workspace` instead.
- Updated all TUI mockups, data flow diagrams, CLI architecture, interaction table, and deferred work.

**Help data** (`apps/cli/src/cli/help-data/workspace.ts`):
- Renamed `unlink` to `remove` in commands, examples, notes
- Updated `link` description to "Move an installed skill to workspace"
- Added `--accept-my-change` flag
- Removed `--source`, `--path`, `--branch` flags (link no longer needs them)

**llms.txt** (`apps/web/public/llms.txt`):
- Updated overview bullet, examples, and all 6 command reference sections to match new names and flags.

<timestamp>Wednesday, May 13, 2026, 1:41 PM (UTC+8)</timestamp>
<user_query>
需要安装什么依赖么？处理 git 和冲突
</user_query>

Good question. Let me check what's already available in the codebase.

The user is asking whether we need to install any new dependencies to handle the git operations (clone, push, pull, force-push) and conflict resolution for the workspace feature.

Let me check what git utilities are already available in the codebase - the CLI already does extensive git operations for `skills add`, `plugins add`, caching, etc.

不需要安装任何新依赖。原因:

1. **Git 操作**: `src/git.ts` 已经封装了完整的 Git 子进程调用层 — `execGit`/`runGit` 通过 Node.js 内置的 `child_process.spawn` 调用系统 `git` 命令。`cloneRepository`、`resolveRemoteRef`、`resolveHeadSha`、`checkoutCommit` 都已实现。workspace 的 push/pull 需要的 `git add`、`git commit`、`git push`、`git push --force-with-lease` 都可以直接用 `execGit` 调。

2. **冲突检测**: push 失败时 `git push` 返回非零退出码，`runGit` 会抛出 `GitCommandError`，stderr 里会包含 `rejected`/`non-fast-forward` 之类的文本。用已有的 `classifyGitError` 扩展一个新的 kind (比如 `'rejected'`) 就够了。

3. **文件复制/diff**: `node:fs/promises` 的 `cp`、`rm`、`readdir`、`readFile` 都是 Node 内置的，已经在 installer 模块里大量使用。status 检测（local vs remote diff）可以用 `execGit(['diff', '--stat'])` 或者逐文件 hash 比较。

4. **TUI**: `@clack/prompts` (selector、confirm、text input、spinner) 和 `picocolors` 已经是依赖了。

总结: workspace 功能用现有的 `git.ts` + Node.js 内置 `fs` + 已有的 `@clack/prompts` 就能完全实现，**零新依赖**。

All 12 todos complete. Here's the summary:

**TDD slices (each RED → GREEN):**
1. **Types** — `WorkspaceSkillEntry` + `workspace: { skills: [] }` field on `AiPackageManifest`
2. **Parser** — `parseWorkspaceSkillEntry`, validation for missing `local`/`source`/`path`, github/gitlab-only constraint, exclusivity check (a name can't be in both `skills` and `workspace.skills`)
3. **Serializer** — emits `workspace.skills` sorted alphabetically
4. **Store** — `addWorkspaceSkill`, `removeWorkspaceSkill`, `moveSkillToWorkspace` + `emptyManifest()` helper
5. **`workspace list`** — `formatWorkspaceList`, `formatWorkspaceListJson`, TTY/AI/JSON output modes
6. **`workspace remove`** — `resolveRemoveTarget`, confirmation prompt, `--yes`, deletes both manifest entry and local directory
7. **`workspace link`** — `discoverInstalledPaths` (scans every agent's `projectSkillsDir`), `buildLinkEntry`, agent-aware selector with custom path fallback and re-prompt on missing path
8. **`workspace push`** — `resolveCloneUrl`, `defaultCommitMessage`, `isPushRejected`; clone → copy → commit → push; conflict handling with TTY selector and `--accept-my-change` for force push
9. **`workspace pull`** — `computeNewVersion`; clone latest → diff local → copy → update SHA; warns and prompts on local changes, `--force` to skip
10. **`workspace status`** — `classifyWorkspaceStatus`, `formatWorkspaceStatus`; checks `clean`/`modified`/`untracked` per skill via shallow clone + `git diff --no-index`
11. **CLI registration** — `registerWorkspaceCommand` wired into `registerCoreCommands` with `ws` alias

**New files (10):**
- `apps/cli/src/commands/workspace/{index,types,list,remove,link,push,pull,status,git-ops}.ts`
- `apps/cli/tests/unit/workspace-manifest.test.ts`
- `apps/cli/tests/unit/workspace-store.test.ts`
- `apps/cli/tests/unit/workspace-commands.test.ts`

**Modified files:**
- `apps/cli/src/types.ts` — added `WorkspaceSkillEntry` and extended `AiPackageManifest`
- `apps/cli/src/manifest/{types,parse,store}.ts` — workspace parsing/serializing/mutations + `emptyManifest()` helper
- `apps/cli/src/commands/index.ts` — register workspace command
- `apps/cli/src/commands/skills/{update,vercel-migrate}.ts` — spread existing manifest instead of rebuilding (required by new `workspace` field)
- 4 unit test files — added `workspace: { skills: [] }` to manifest fixtures

**Validation:** type-check pass, 155 unit tests pass (36 new workspace tests), 26 e2e tests pass, biome clean, build success.

**Zero new dependencies** — workspace uses the existing `execGit` (`node:child_process`), `@clack/prompts`, and `node:fs/promises` infrastructure.

Note: I implemented `link`, `remove`, `push`, `pull`, `status`, and `list` per the design doc. The `skills add --workspace` flag was not yet wired into `skills/add.ts` (it requires extending the existing skills add flow with a workspace branch — let me know if you want me to add that next).

# AI Skills Labs — AI Component Marketplace & CLI

> AI Skills Labs is a unified platform for distributing and discovering AI components (Skills, MCP Servers, Plugins) across 47+ AI coding assistants. The `@tiktok-fe/ai` CLI (binary: `ai`) provides installation, publishing, version tracking, and multi-Agent distribution.

## Marketplace

- [Marketplace Overview](/llms/marketplace/overview.txt): Component types, common operations, installation format
- [Skills](/llms/marketplace/skill.txt): Portable Agent capability packages (SKILL.md format, three-tier loading, directory structure)
- [MCP Servers](/llms/marketplace/mcp-server.txt): Model Context Protocol configurations (stdio/SSE/HTTP transports, deep link install)
- [Plugins](/llms/marketplace/plugin.txt): Multi-component bundles (plugin.json manifest, auto-discovery, multi-Agent support)

## CLI Commands

### Overview & Configuration

- [CLI Overview](/llms/commands/overview.txt): Quick start, ai-package.json traceability, dual mode design, global flags, full command tree
- [Configuration Reference](/llms/commands/configuration.txt): Complete config.json key reference (display, agent detection/selection/skip, install settings)
- [Environment Variables](/llms/commands/environment.txt): AI_CLI_BASE, AI_CLI_CONFIG, GITHUB_TOKEN, CODEBASE_TOKEN, resolution priority

### Top-level Commands

- [setup](/llms/commands/setup.txt): Five-step interactive config wizard (language, ai-package.json, Agent detection, defaults, skip)
- [login](/llms/commands/login.txt): Browser SSO / QR code authentication
- [whoami](/llms/commands/whoami.txt): Current user info (--json for structured output)
- [config](/llms/commands/config.txt): get / set / reset / where config management (alias: cfg)
- [install](/llms/commands/install.txt): Batch install from ai-package.json (alias: i)
- [update](/llms/commands/update.txt): Check and apply remote updates (alias: up)
- [agents](/llms/commands/agents.txt): List 47 supported Agents with capability status

### Skill Commands (`ai skill ...`)

- [skill](/llms/commands/skill/index.txt): Skill command group overview
- [skill add](/llms/commands/skill/add.txt): Install from GitHub/Codebase/registry with security audit
- [skill list](/llms/commands/skill/list.txt): List installed skills grouped by Agent
- [skill remove](/llms/commands/skill/remove.txt): Remove a skill and clean up ai-package.json
- [skill clean](/llms/commands/skill/clean.txt): Remove all installed skills
- [skill init](/llms/commands/skill/init.txt): Create SKILL.md template
- [skill publish](/llms/commands/skill/publish.txt): Package and publish to registry
- [skill unpublish](/llms/commands/skill/unpublish.txt): Unpublish from registry

### MCP Commands (`ai mcp ...`)

- [mcp](/llms/commands/mcp/index.txt): MCP command group overview
- [mcp add](/llms/commands/mcp/add.txt): Install from registry/GitHub/Codebase
- [mcp list](/llms/commands/mcp/list.txt): List configured MCP servers
- [mcp remove](/llms/commands/mcp/remove.txt): Remove an MCP server configuration

### Plugin Commands (`ai plugin ...`)

  skills: WorkspaceSkillEntry[]
): string =>

      name: skill.name,
      local: skill.local,
      source: skill.source,
      path: skill.path,
      version: skill.version,

The validator requires `gitlab:https://` format. Let me use the git `insteadOf` trick (already done in install.e2e.test.ts) to redirect a fake HTTPS URL to the local file:// repo.

 I'm running into an issue where the GitLab validator rejects `file://` URLs, so I can't use `gitlab:file://` as a source. I'm weighing a few approaches: either relax the validation during workspace parsing (though that compromises consistency), use a real GitLab URL with a local git config override like the e2e tests do, or add `file:` support specifically for the workspace push/pull tests.

Looking at the validator more closely, it only accepts `https?://` or `git@` patterns, so `file://` doesn't match either. The best solution is to borrow the `insteadOf` trick from install.e2e.test.ts — I'll map a fake HTTPS URL to the local file path through a gitconfig file passed via `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL`. This works for clones, but push operations from the cloned working tree are trickier since the `insteadOf` mapping doesn't automatically apply to the remote URL stored in the repo's config.

Actually, git's `insteadOf` substitution happens at URL interpretation time, so when pushing, git would apply the same mapping and redirect to the file URL. I can use this approach by setting the manifest source to a fake HTTPS URL and creating a git config that redirects it to the local file path. Let me rewrite the push/pull test with this strategy.

# AI SKILLS / ai-pkgs

> ai-pkgs is a package manager for AI agent skills, plugins, and workspace
> (local skill iteration with Git push/pull). It installs skills into
> agent-specific directories, plugins into vendor-specific caches for Claude
> Code / Cursor / Codex, and tracks every entry in `ai-package.json` for
> reproducible installs.

## Overview

- [Overview](/llms/overview.txt): What ai-pkgs does, ai-package.json shape, dual mode design (TTY vs `--ai`), global flags
- [Sources](/llms/sources.txt): GitHub / GitLab / file / Marketplace registries and source locator syntax
- [Agent targets](/llms/agent-targets.txt): 47+ supported AI coding assistants with project / global skill directory conventions
- [Cache](/llms/cache.txt): Global Git cache (`~/.cache/ai-pkgs`) and `cache clear` options

## Top-level commands

- [install](/llms/install.txt): Restore every skill and plugin declared in `ai-package.json`

## Skill commands (`ai-pkgs skills ...`)

- [skills](/llms/skills/index.txt): Skill command group overview
- [skills add](/llms/skills/add.txt): Resolve a source, discover SKILL.md, write the manifest, install into agent targets
- [skills list](/llms/skills/list.txt): List manifest skills as grouped text or `--json`
- [skills remove](/llms/skills/remove.txt): Remove skill entries from `ai-package.json`
- [skills outdated](/llms/skills/outdated.txt): Report-only check for newer Git commits
- [skills update](/llms/skills/update.txt): Refresh pinned Git SHAs with explicit confirmation
- [skills vercel-migrate](/llms/skills/vercel-migrate.txt): Migrate legacy `skills-lock.json` into `ai-package.json`

## Plugin commands (`ai-pkgs plugins ...`)

- [plugins](/llms/plugins/index.txt): Plugin command group overview, per-plugin `targets` model
- [plugins init](/llms/plugins/init.txt): Scaffold a plugin template with vendor manifests and `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json`
- [plugins add](/llms/plugins/add.txt): Discover plugins in a source, select target agents, install, write the manifest
- [plugins list](/llms/plugins/list.txt): List manifest plugins as grouped text or `--json`
- [plugins targets](/llms/plugins/targets.txt): Manage per-plugin agent targets (add / remove / list)
- [plugins outdated](/llms/plugins/outdated.txt): Report-only check for newer Git commits
- [plugins update](/llms/plugins/update.txt): Refresh pinned Git SHAs
- [plugins remove](/llms/plugins/remove.txt): Remove plugin entries, optionally also uninstall from agent caches

## Workspace commands (`ai-pkgs workspace ...` / `ws`)

- [workspace](/llms/workspace/index.txt): Workspace command group overview and `ai-package.json` shape
- [workspace link](/llms/workspace/link.txt): Move an installed skill into `workspace.skills` for local iteration
- [workspace remove](/llms/workspace/remove.txt): Remove workspace entry and delete local files
- [workspace push](/llms/workspace/push.txt): Push local skill changes to remote (with `--accept-my-change` for force push)
- [workspace pull](/llms/workspace/pull.txt): Pull the latest version from remote into local
- [workspace status](/llms/workspace/status.txt): Show clean / modified / untracked state per workspace skill
- [workspace list](/llms/workspace/list.txt): List workspace skill entries

## Guides

- [Supported agents](/llms/agent-targets.txt): Full list of supported AI coding assistants with their skill directories
- [Common workflows](/llms/workflows.txt): Recipes for ai-package.json restore, fork-iterate-publish, and CI installs
