input.contents:
# ai-pkgs overview

ai-pkgs is a package manager for reusable AI agent components. It tracks every
installed item in `ai-package.json` for reproducible setups across machines,
CI, and teammates.

## What ai-pkgs does

- Restores project-declared skills and plugins from `ai-package.json`.
- Adds skills from GitHub, GitLab, local files, and a future Marketplace
  provider boundary.
- Adds plugins (multi-artifact bundles with skills, commands, agents, rules,
  hooks, MCP configs) from the same source types. Each manifest entry carries
  a `targets` field listing which agents the plugin installs to.
- Iterates on skills locally with the `workspace` section: link an installed
  skill, edit it in place, push changes back to its Git remote, pull updates,
  and check the modified/clean/untracked state.
- Manages per-plugin agent targets with `plugins targets add|remove|list`.
- Scaffolds new plugin templates with `plugins init`, generating
  `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` as the universal plugin format.
- Lists declared manifest entries as grouped text or `--json`.
- Reports outdated Git-backed skills and plugins without mutating
  `ai-package.json`.
- Updates all or selected Git-backed pins with explicit confirmation.
- Pins Git sources as `<ref>@<resolvedSha>` for reproducible installs.
- Installs skills by copying directories by default, or symlinking with
  `--link`.
- Installs plugins into vendor-specific caches (Claude plugin cache, Cursor
  extensions, Codex plugin cache) with env-var translation.
- Enables project-scope plugins for Claude Code by writing `enabledPlugins`
  to `.claude/settings.json` in the project root.
- Supports project-local and global target directories for `skills add`.
- Supports a fixed global manifest at `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json` with
  `-g, --global`.
- Uses a global Git cache keyed by provider, source, and commit SHA so
  repeated installs can reuse the same checkout.
- Provides `--ai` for strict non-interactive automation with deterministic
  clack-style text output.

## ai-package.json shape

```json
{
  "skills": {
    "tdd": {
      "source": "github:mattpocock/skills",
      "version": "main@b843cb5...",
      "path": "skills/engineering/tdd"
    }
  },
  "plugins": {
    "vercel-plugin": {
      "source": "github:vercel/vercel-plugin",
      "version": "main@abc1234...",
      "path": ".",
      "targets": ["claude-code", "cursor"]
    }
  },
  "workspace": {
    "skills": {
      "explain": {
        "local": ".cursor/skills/explain",
        "source": "github:entireio/skills",
        "path": "skills/explain",
        "version": "main@c376dc9..."
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Each entry uses `<ref>@<commitSha>` as its `version` for reproducibility.
At least one of `skills`, `plugins`, or `workspace` must be present.

## Dual mode design

ai-pkgs detects two execution modes and degrades gracefully:

- **TTY mode** (default in an interactive terminal): clack-style intro / outro,
  spinners, selectors, and confirmation prompts.
- **`--ai` / non-TTY mode**: deterministic plain-text output suitable for AI
  agents, CI, and scripts. All prompts are disabled. Pass `--yes`, `--agent`,
  and other selection flags up front when needed.

## Global flags

- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode for automation
- `-h, --help`: show help for the current command
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory containing `ai-package.json`
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: alternative manifest path (mutually exclusive with
  `--global` and `--install-only`)
- `-g, --global`: use `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json` instead of the project
  manifest

## Help command tree

```bash
ai-pkgs --help
ai-pkgs skills -h
ai-pkgs plugins -h
ai-pkgs workspace -h
ai-pkgs help skills add
ai-pkgs help plugins add
ai-pkgs help cache clear
```

# Source registries and locator syntax

ai-pkgs reads sources via a pluggable registry boundary. The CLI auto-detects
the registry from the source string, or it can be forced with `--registry`.

## Source locator format

Manifest entries store `source` as `<provider>:<package-id>`:

- `github:owner/repo`
- `gitlab:https://host/group/repo.git`
- `gitlab:git@host:owner/repo.git`
- `file:./relative-path`
- `marketplace:owner/package` (reserved, not yet implemented)

## CLI argument forms

GitHub is the default registry:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add owner/repo
```

Explicit source forms:

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add github:owner/repo
ai-pkgs skills add https://github.com/owner/repo --registry github
ai-pkgs skills add https://gitlab.example.com/group/repo.git --registry gitlab
ai-pkgs skills add ./local-skills --registry file
ai-pkgs skills add file:./local-skills
```

## Registries

- **github**: owner/repo shorthand or full https URL. Always resolves via
  `git ls-remote` and clones with `--no-checkout` for SHA-pinned reproducibility.
- **gitlab**: requires a full clone URL (https or `git@`) so self-hosted
  origins are preserved.
- **file**: local path. No Git operations; the manifest stores `file:` as
  the source so installs can be re-materialized later.
- **marketplace**: provider boundary reserved for the future marketplace API.

## Git cache

All Git-backed sources share a global cache at `~/.cache/ai-pkgs` keyed by
provider, source, and resolved commit SHA. See [cache](./cache.txt) for the
cache layout and `cache clear` flags.

# Agent targets

ai-pkgs supports 47+ AI coding assistants. Each target maps to a project
skill directory and a global skill directory. `skills add` writes copies (or
symlinks with `--link`) into the chosen target's directory.

## Common targets

- `cursor`: project `.cursor/skills`, global `~/.cursor/skills`
- `claude-code`: project `.claude/skills`, global `~/.claude/skills`
- `codex`: project `.codex/skills`, global `~/.codex/skills`
- `universal`: project `.agents/skills`, global `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-~/.config}/agents/skills`

## All supported agents

Run `ai-pkgs skills add <source>` and pick from the interactive selector, or
pass `--agent <id>` repeatedly. The full list (with project skill paths) is:

```
adal               .adal/skills
aider-desk         .aider-desk/skills
amp                .agents/skills
antigravity        .agents/skills
augment            .augment/skills
bob                .bob/skills
claude-code        .claude/skills
cline              .agents/skills
codearts-agent     .codeartsdoer/skills
codebuddy          .codebuddy/skills
codemaker          .codemaker/skills
codestudio         .codestudio/skills
codex              .codex/skills
command-code       .commandcode/skills
continue           .continue/skills
cortex             .cortex/skills
crush              .crush/skills
cursor             .cursor/skills
deepagents         .agents/skills
devin              .devin/skills
dexto              .agents/skills
droid              .factory/skills
firebender         .agents/skills
forgecode          .forge/skills
gemini-cli         .agents/skills
github-copilot     .agents/skills
goose              .goose/skills
iflow-cli          .iflow/skills
junie              .junie/skills
kilo               .kilocode/skills
kimi-cli           .agents/skills
kiro-cli           .kiro/skills
kode               .kode/skills
mcpjam             .mcpjam/skills
mistral-vibe       .vibe/skills
mux                .mux/skills
neovate            .neovate/skills
openclaw           skills
opencode           .agents/skills
openhands          .openhands/skills
pi                 .pi/skills
pochi              .pochi/skills
qoder              .qoder/skills
qwen-code          .qwen/skills
roo                .roo/skills
rovodev            .rovodev/skills
tabnine-cli        .tabnine/agent/skills
trae               .trae/skills
trae-cn            .trae/skills
warp               .agents/skills
windsurf           .windsurf/skills
zencoder           .zencoder/skills
```

## Plugin-capable agents

Only three agents currently support plugins:

- `claude-code`: cache at `~/.claude/plugins/cache/`, settings at
  `~/.claude/settings.json` (global) or `.claude/settings.json` (project).
- `cursor`: shares the Claude plugin cache on macOS / Linux; uses
  `~/.cursor/extensions/` on Windows.
- `codex`: cache at `~/.codex/plugins/cache/`, registers each plugin in
  `~/.codex/config.toml` and `~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json`.

See [plugins/add](./plugins/add.txt) for the target selector and
[plugins/targets](./plugins/targets.txt) for per-plugin target management.

# Git cache

ai-pkgs caches every Git-backed clone under `~/.cache/ai-pkgs` keyed by
provider, source, and resolved commit SHA. `skills add`, `plugins add`,
`workspace push`, `workspace pull`, and `install` all reuse the same cache
to avoid repeated network round-trips.

Override the cache root with the `AI_PKGS_CACHE_HOME` environment variable
(useful in CI and tests).

## `ai-pkgs cache clear [options]`

Clears all cached Git checkouts, or a filtered subset.

Flags:

- `--provider <provider>`: filter by `github` or `gitlab`
- `--source <source>`: filter by source, such as `owner/repo` or a full clone URL
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs cache clear
ai-pkgs cache clear --provider github
ai-pkgs cache clear --provider github --source vercel-labs/skills
ai-pkgs cache clear --ai --yes
```

# `ai-pkgs install [options]`

Restores every skill and plugin declared in `ai-package.json`. Skills install
into project-local agent targets by default. Plugins install to the agents
listed in each entry's `targets` array — no agent picker is shown because
targets are already persisted. With `--global`, it reads
`~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json` and installs into global agent targets.

Workspace skills (`workspace.skills`) are NOT installed by `install`; they
are already at their `local` path on disk. Use `workspace pull` to sync
them from the remote.

## Flags

- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory to install into
- `-a, --agent <agent>`: target agent; repeat or pass comma-separated
- `--copy`: copy skill directories (default)
- `--link`: symlink skill directories
- `--force`: overwrite existing skill directories
- `--skip-existing`: skip existing skill directories
- `--project`: explicit project-local install dimension
- `-g, --global`: restore the global manifest into global agent directories
- `--refresh`: refresh the Git cache before installing
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompts
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode
- `-h, --help`: show help

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs install --agent cursor --force --yes
ai-pkgs install --link --agent claude-code
ai-pkgs install --global --yes
ai-pkgs --ai install --agent cursor --force --yes
```

## TTY behavior

When stdin is a TTY and `--ai` is not set, `install` prints a confirmation
plan as a clack note before proceeding. Without `--yes`, the user is asked
to confirm. In `--ai` or non-TTY mode, `--yes` is required to proceed.

content.text: Now the skills group.

input.contents:
# `ai-pkgs skills` — skill commands

Skills are SKILL.md-bearing directories. ai-pkgs reads them from any
configured source, writes manifest entries with pinned versions, and installs
them into per-agent skill directories.

## Subcommands

- [skills add](./add.txt): resolve a source, discover SKILL.md, write manifest, install into agent targets
- [skills list](./list.txt): list manifest skills as grouped text or `--json`
- [skills remove](./remove.txt): remove skill entries from `ai-package.json`
- [skills outdated](./outdated.txt): report-only check for newer Git commits
- [skills update](./update.txt): refresh pinned Git SHAs with explicit confirmation
- [skills vercel-migrate](./vercel-migrate.txt): migrate legacy `skills-lock.json`
- skills search: reserved for the future marketplace API

## Common flags

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: alternative manifest path
- `-g, --global`: read/write `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompts
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode
- `-h, --help`: show help

# `ai-pkgs skills add <source> [options]`

Resolves a source, discovers `SKILL.md` directories, selects skills, writes
selected entries to `ai-package.json`, and installs them into agent skill
directories. Use `--install-only` for one-off installs with no manifest reads
or writes.

## Source flags

- `--registry <registry>`: `github`, `gitlab`, `marketplace`, or `file`
- `--ref <ref>`: Git branch, tag, or commit to pin
- `--path <path>`: subdirectory to scan inside the source
- `-s, --skill <skill>`: select a skill by name (repeatable)
- `--all`: select all discovered skills (mutually exclusive with `--skill`)
- `--refresh`: refresh Git cache before installing

## Install flags

- `-a, --agent <agent>`: target agent (repeatable)
- `--project`: install into project-local agent skill directories
- `-g, --global`: write `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json` and install globally
- `--copy`: copy skill directories (default)
- `--link`: symlink skill directories
- `--force`: overwrite existing skill directories
- `--skip-existing`: skip existing skill directories
- `--install-only`: install without writing `ai-package.json`
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompts
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Project flags

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json` (incompatible with
  `--install-only` and `--global`)

## TTY behavior

- If no `--agent` is supplied and prompts are allowed, ai-pkgs opens a
  searchable agent picker.
- If no `--project` or `--global` is supplied, TTY mode prompts for the
  install dimension.
- Multi-skill selection supports type-to-filter, arrow keys, space to
  select, `a` to toggle all matching skills, and enter to confirm.

## Non-interactive behavior

- In non-TTY and `--ai` mode, omitted scope defaults to project.
- `skills add --global --install-only` installs globally without writing
  `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`.
- In `--ai` mode, all prompts are disabled. Pass `--agent`, `--all` or
  `--skill`, `--force` or `--skip-existing`, and `--yes` as needed.

## Examples

```bash
# Add all discovered skills from a GitHub repository
ai-pkgs skills add vercel-labs/skills --all --agent cursor --project

# Add a single skill globally
ai-pkgs skills add vercel-labs/skills --global --skill tdd --agent cursor --force --yes

# Add from an explicit GitHub URL with a source subdirectory
ai-pkgs skills add https://github.com/mattpocock/skills \
  --path skills \
  --skill tdd \
  --agent cursor \
  --project

# Install directly without writing the manifest
ai-pkgs skills add vercel-labs/skills \
  --install-only \
  --all \
  --agent cursor \
  --force

# Non-interactive automation
ai-pkgs --ai skills add vercel-labs/skills \
  --install-only \
  --all \
  --agent cursor \
  --project \
  --force \
  --yes
```

# `ai-pkgs skills list [options]`

Lists skills declared in `ai-package.json`.

## Flags

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-g, --global`: read `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `--json`: print machine-readable manifest skill entries
- `--ai`: plain-text output

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs skills list
ai-pkgs skills list --json
ai-pkgs skills list --global
ai-pkgs skills list --ai
```

# `ai-pkgs skills remove <skill...> [options]`

Removes manifest entries by name. Installed skill folders on disk are left
untouched. To also delete files, use `workspace remove` (for workspace
skills) or remove the agent directory manually.

Workspace skills cannot be removed via `skills remove`. Use
`workspace remove` instead.

## Flags

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-g, --global`: edit `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs skills remove tdd
ai-pkgs skills remove tdd to-prd --yes
ai-pkgs skills remove tdd --global
```

# `ai-pkgs skills outdated [skill...] [options]`

Checks all or selected manifest skills for newer Git commits by resolving
each stored `ref` and comparing it with `commitSha`. Outdated results are
report-only and exit 0; failed remote checks exit 1.

## Flags

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-g, --global`: check `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `--ai`: plain-text output

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs skills outdated
ai-pkgs skills outdated tdd to-prd
ai-pkgs skills outdated --ai
```

# `ai-pkgs skills update [skill...] [options]`

Refreshes pinned Git SHAs for all or selected Git-backed manifest entries.
TTY mode asks before writing; non-TTY and `--ai` require `--yes`. If any
requested check fails, the command exits 1 and does not write a partial
manifest.

## Flags

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-g, --global`: update `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `-y, --yes`: confirm writes without prompting
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs skills update --yes
ai-pkgs skills update tdd --yes
ai-pkgs skills update --global --yes
```

# `ai-pkgs skills vercel-migrate [options]`

Migrates a legacy Vercel-style `skills-lock.json` (or compatible lockfile)
into `ai-package.json`. Resolves each lockfile entry to a manifest skill
entry with a pinned `<ref>@<commitSha>` version.

## Flags

- `--lockfile <path>`: path to the legacy `skills-lock.json`
- `--remove-lock`: remove the lockfile after a successful migration
- `--install`: install the migrated manifest after writing
- `--force`: overwrite existing manifest entries on collision
- `--skip-existing`: skip existing manifest entries on collision
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompts
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs skills vercel-migrate
ai-pkgs skills vercel-migrate --lockfile ./skills-lock.json --remove-lock --install
ai-pkgs skills vercel-migrate --force --yes
```

content.text: Now the plugins group.

input.contents:
# `ai-pkgs plugins` — plugin commands

Plugins are multi-artifact bundles: skills, commands, agents, rules, hooks,
MCP configs, and vendor-specific manifests (`.claude-plugin/plugin.json`,
`.cursor-plugin/plugin.json`, `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, or the universal
`.agents/plugins/marketplace.json`).

Each manifest entry carries a `targets` array recording which agents the
plugin installs to. `ai-pkgs install` reuses these targets so plugins are
restored to the same agents that were originally selected.

## Subcommands

- [plugins init](./init.txt): scaffold a plugin template with vendor manifests
- [plugins add](./add.txt): discover plugins in a source, select targets, install, write manifest
- [plugins list](./list.txt): list manifest plugins as grouped text or `--json`
- [plugins targets](./targets.txt): manage per-plugin agent targets (add / remove / list)
- [plugins outdated](./outdated.txt): report-only check for newer Git commits
- [plugins update](./update.txt): refresh pinned Git SHAs
- [plugins remove](./remove.txt): remove plugin entries, optionally uninstall

## Plugin-capable agents

Only three agents currently support plugins:

| Target | Cache | Settings |
|--------|-------|----------|
| `claude-code` | `~/.claude/plugins/cache/` | global `~/.claude/settings.json` or project `.claude/settings.json` |
| `cursor` | shares Claude cache on macOS/Linux; `~/.cursor/extensions/` on Windows | n/a |
| `codex` | `~/.codex/plugins/cache/` | `~/.codex/config.toml` and `~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` |

## Common flags

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: alternative manifest path
- `-g, --global`: read/write `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompts
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode
- `-h, --help`: show help

# `ai-pkgs plugins init [name] [options]`

Scaffolds a new plugin directory with vendor-specific manifests and component
stubs. Always generates `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` as the universal
plugin format alongside any vendor-specific manifests.

Interactive prompts ask for:

- Plugin name
- Target directory
- Target agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex)
- Components (skills, commands, agents, rules, hooks, `.mcp.json`)

## Generated structure (all agents, all components)

```
my-plugin/
  .agents/
    plugins/
      marketplace.json
  .claude-plugin/
    plugin.json
  .cursor-plugin/
    plugin.json
  .codex-plugin/
    plugin.json
  skills/
    example-skill/
      SKILL.md
  commands/
    example-command.md
  agents/
    example-agent.md
  rules/
    example-rule.mdc
  hooks/
    hooks.json
  .mcp.json
  README.md
```

## Flags

- `-a, --agent <agent>`: pre-select target agents, skip prompt (repeatable)
- `-y, --yes`: use all defaults (all agents, all components)
- `--force`: overwrite existing directory without prompting
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode (requires name + agents passed up front)

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins init my-plugin
ai-pkgs plugins init my-plugin --agent claude-code --yes
ai-pkgs plugins init my-plugin --agent claude-code --agent codex --force --ai
```

# `ai-pkgs plugins add <source> [options]`

Discovers plugins in a source repo, presents a target agent selector,
installs to the selected agents, and writes entries (including the persisted
`targets` array) to the `plugins` key in `ai-package.json`.

The target selector detects vendor directories (`.claude-plugin/`,
`.cursor-plugin/`, `.codex-plugin/`, `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json`) to
determine compatible agents. For project-scope installs targeting Claude
Code, also writes `enabledPlugins` to `.claude/settings.json` in the project
root.

## Flags

- `--registry <registry>`: `github`, `gitlab`, `marketplace`, or `file`
- `--ref <ref>`: Git branch, tag, or commit to pin
- `--path <path>`: subdirectory to scan inside the source
- `-p, --plugin <plugin>`: plugin name to select (repeatable)
- `--refresh`: refresh Git cache before installing
- `-a, --agent <agent>`: target agent; skips the selector (repeatable)
- `--scope <scope>`: install scope: user, project, local
- `--install-only`: install without writing `ai-package.json`
- `-g, --global`: write `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `--verbose`: show per-target install progress
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompts
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Target selector behavior

| Found in plugin | Targets enabled |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` | All (claude-code, cursor, codex) |
| `.claude-plugin/` only | claude-code, cursor (cursor translates) |
| `.cursor-plugin/` only | cursor |
| `.codex-plugin/` only | codex |
| `.claude-plugin/` + `.codex-plugin/` | all |
| No vendor dir but has `skills/`, `commands/`, etc. | all (installer generates vendor dirs) |

Targets without vendor support are shown as disabled with a reason hint.
`--agent <id>` skips the selector entirely.

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins add vercel/vercel-plugin --yes
ai-pkgs plugins add ./my-plugin --plugin my-plugin
ai-pkgs plugins add owner/repo --agent claude-code --agent cursor --yes
ai-pkgs plugins add owner/repo --install-only --agent codex --yes
```

# `ai-pkgs plugins list [options]`

Lists plugins declared in `ai-package.json`, including the `targets` array
for each entry.

## Flags

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-g, --global`: read `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `--json`: print machine-readable manifest plugin entries
- `--ai`: plain-text output

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins list
ai-pkgs plugins list --json
ai-pkgs plugins list --global
```

# `ai-pkgs plugins targets <add|remove|list> <plugin> [agent...]`

Manage per-plugin target agents in `ai-package.json`.

- `targets add` updates the manifest and prompts to install for any newly
  added agents.
- `targets remove` updates the manifest and prompts to uninstall from any
  removed agents.
- `targets list` shows current targets.

Valid agents: `claude-code`, `cursor`, `codex`.

## Flags

- `-y, --yes`: auto-confirm install / uninstall
- `--no-install`: skip the install / uninstall prompt entirely
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-g, --global`: edit `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins targets add vercel-plugin cursor
ai-pkgs plugins targets add vercel-plugin cursor codex --yes
ai-pkgs plugins targets remove vercel-plugin codex
ai-pkgs plugins targets list vercel-plugin
ai-pkgs plugins targets add vercel-plugin cursor --no-install
```

# `ai-pkgs plugins outdated [plugin...] [options]`

Checks all or selected manifest plugins for newer Git commits. Report-only:
outdated results exit 0; failed remote checks exit 1.

## Flags

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-g, --global`: check `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `--ai`: plain-text output

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins outdated
ai-pkgs plugins outdated vercel-plugin
ai-pkgs plugins outdated --ai
```

# `ai-pkgs plugins update [plugin...] [options]`

Refreshes pinned Git SHAs for all or selected Git-backed plugin entries.
After writing the new SHAs to `ai-package.json`, re-installs each updated
plugin to its persisted `targets` so cache directories and agent settings
catch up.

## Flags

- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-g, --global`: update `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `-y, --yes`: confirm writes without prompting
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins update --yes
ai-pkgs plugins update vercel-plugin --yes
ai-pkgs plugins update --global --yes
```

# `ai-pkgs plugins remove <plugin...> [options]`

Removes plugin entries from `ai-package.json`. With `--uninstall`, also
cleans agent plugin cache directories (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) and
removes the `enabledPlugins` keys from agent settings.

## Flags

- `--uninstall`: also clean agent plugin cache directories
- `-a, --agent <agent>`: scope uninstall to specific agents
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `-m, --manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `-g, --global`: edit `~/.ai-pkgs/ai-package.json`
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins remove vercel-plugin
ai-pkgs plugins remove vercel-plugin --uninstall
ai-pkgs plugins remove vercel-plugin --uninstall --agent claude-code --yes
```

content.text: Now the workspace group.

input.contents:
# `ai-pkgs workspace` — local skill iteration

Workspace turns ai-pkgs into a two-way Git client for skills you author or
modify locally. Link an installed skill, edit it in place, push changes
back to its Git remote, pull updates, and check the modified / clean /
untracked state.

Alias: `ws` (e.g. `ai-pkgs ws push explain`).

## Subcommands

- [workspace link](./link.txt): move an installed skill into `workspace.skills`
- [workspace remove](./remove.txt): remove workspace entry and delete local files
- [workspace push](./push.txt): push local changes to remote
- [workspace pull](./pull.txt): pull latest from remote into local
- [workspace status](./status.txt): show clean / modified / untracked per skill
- [workspace list](./list.txt): list workspace skill entries

## Manifest shape

```json
{
  "workspace": {
    "skills": {
      "explain": {
        "local": ".cursor/skills/explain",
        "source": "github:entireio/skills",
        "path": "skills/explain",
        "version": "main@c376dc9..."
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Field rules

- `local`: relative path from project root to the skill directory on disk
- `source`: Git remote, same format as `skills[].source` (github / gitlab /
  raw clone URL). User must have push access.
- `path`: directory inside the remote repo containing this skill
- `version`: `<branch>@<commitSha>`. Branch is locked at link time; SHA
  updates after each push or pull.

### Exclusivity

A skill name is in `skills` OR `workspace.skills`, never both. `workspace
link` moves an existing dependency into workspace mode. `workspace remove`
deletes both the workspace entry and the local skill directory.

`skills add`, `skills list`, `skills update`, and `skills outdated` ignore
workspace skills. `ai-pkgs install` does NOT install workspace skills (they
are already at their `local` path) — use `workspace pull` to sync from
remote.

## Common flags

- `--local <path>`: local skill path on disk (link)
- `-m, --message <msg>`: commit message (push)
- `--accept-my-change`: force push when remote has diverged (push)
- `--force`: skip overwrite confirmation (pull)
- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompts (remove)
- `--json`: machine-readable output (list, status)
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `--manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

# `ai-pkgs workspace link <name> [options]`

Moves an installed skill from `skills[]` into `workspace.skills` for local
iteration. Only works for skills already in the manifest; auto-fills
`source`, `path`, and `version` from the existing entry.

Prompts only for the local path with an agent-aware selector (scans every
agent's project skill directory for a folder matching `<name>`). A
"Custom path" option opens a text input that re-prompts on missing paths.

For new skills, use `skills add <source> --workspace` instead.

## Flags

- `--local <path>`: local skill path on disk (skip selector; required in `--ai` mode)
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `--manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Errors

- Skill not in `skills[]`: `"<name>" is not in skills. Use "skills add <source> --workspace" to add a new workspace skill.`
- Skill already in workspace: `"<name>" is already a workspace skill.`
- `--ai` without `--local`: `--local is required when linking in non-interactive mode.`
- `--local` path does not exist: `Path does not exist: <local>`

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs workspace link explain
ai-pkgs workspace link explain --local .cursor/skills/explain
ai-pkgs workspace link explain --local .claude/skills/explain --ai
```

# `ai-pkgs workspace remove <name> [options]`

Removes a workspace skill entry from `ai-package.json` AND deletes the local
skill directory from disk. Destructive — files are permanently removed.

TTY prompts for confirmation; `--ai` requires `--yes` to proceed. If the
local path is already missing, prints a warning and still removes the
manifest entry.

## Flags

- `-y, --yes`: skip confirmation prompt (required in `--ai` mode)
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `--manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Errors

- Skill not in workspace: `"<name>" is not a workspace skill. Available: <names>`
- `--ai` without `--yes`: `Pass --yes to confirm removal in non-interactive mode.`

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs workspace remove explain
ai-pkgs workspace remove explain --yes
ai-pkgs workspace remove explain --yes --ai
```

# `ai-pkgs workspace push <name> [options]`

Pushes local skill changes to the remote Git repository.

## Flow

1. Clone the remote at the locked branch into a temp dir.
2. Copy the local skill directory (`local`) into the clone at `path`
   (full directory overwrite).
3. `git add . && git commit -m "<message>"`.
4. `git push origin <branch>`.
5. On success: update `version` in `ai-package.json` to `<branch>@<newSha>`
   and clean up the temp dir.

## Conflict resolution

When the remote has diverged and `git push` is rejected:

| Mode | No flag | `--accept-my-change` |
|------|---------|---------------------|
| TTY | Show selector: "Accept my changes (force push)" vs "Resolve locally (manual merge)" | Force push without prompt |
| `--ai` | Fail with bail-out message and temp clone path | Force push without prompt |

"Accept my changes" runs `git push --force-with-lease`. "Resolve locally"
preserves the temp clone for manual `git pull --rebase` + `git push`. After
resolving, the user runs `workspace pull` to sync the resolved state back to
their local directory.

## Flags

- `-m, --message <msg>`: commit message
- `--accept-my-change`: force push on rejection (overwrite remote)
- `-y, --yes`: skip commit message prompt (use the default)
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `--manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode (auto-generates commit message)

Default commit message: `chore: update <skill-name>`.

## Errors

- Skill not in workspace: `"<name>" is not a workspace skill.`
- Local path missing: `Local path "<local>" does not exist. Did you move or delete the skill?`
- No changes: `No changes to push: local matches the remote at this commit.`

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs workspace push explain
ai-pkgs workspace push explain -m "feat: improve examples"
ai-pkgs workspace push explain --accept-my-change
ai-pkgs workspace push explain --ai -m "chore: e2e update"
ai-pkgs workspace push explain --accept-my-change --ai
```

# `ai-pkgs workspace pull <name> [options]`

Pulls the latest version of a skill from the remote into the local
directory.

## Flow

1. Clone the remote at the latest commit on the locked branch.
2. Detect local changes by diffing against the pinned `commitSha`.
3. If local has changes and we're in TTY without `--force`, prompt to
   confirm overwrite.
4. Copy the clone's `path` directory into the local directory (full
   replace).
5. Update `version` in `ai-package.json` to `<branch>@<latestSha>`.

`--ai` mode and `--force` always overwrite without prompting. Use
`workspace status` first to check for local changes.

## Flags

- `--force`: skip overwrite confirmation
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `--manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `--ai`: strict non-interactive mode

## Errors

- Skill not in workspace: `"<name>" is not a workspace skill.`
- Remote fetch failed: `Failed to fetch <source>. Check the remote URL and your network connection.`

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs workspace pull explain
ai-pkgs workspace pull explain --force
ai-pkgs workspace pull explain --force --ai
```

# `ai-pkgs workspace status [name]`

Shows the sync state of workspace skills. Clones the remote at the pinned
`commitSha` for each entry (shallow / blobless) and diffs against the local
directory.

## Status values

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `clean` | Local matches the last pushed version |
| `modified` | Local has changes since last push / pull |
| `untracked` | Version is `0000000` (never pushed) |

## Flags

- `--json`: machine-readable output (`name`, `status`, `local`, `version`)
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `--manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `--ai`: plain-text output

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs workspace status
ai-pkgs workspace status explain
ai-pkgs workspace status --json
ai-pkgs workspace status --ai
```

# `ai-pkgs workspace list [options]`

Lists workspace skill entries declared in `ai-package.json`. Output modes:

- TTY (default): clack-style intro / outro with a per-skill note showing
  `local`, `source`, `path`, and `version`.
- `--ai` or non-TTY: stable plain text, one line per skill.
- `--json`: machine-readable JSON array.

## Flags

- `--json`: machine-readable output
- `-C, --dir <path>`: project directory
- `--manifest <path>`: path to `ai-package.json`
- `--ai`: plain-text output

## Examples

```bash
ai-pkgs workspace list
ai-pkgs workspace list --ai
ai-pkgs workspace list --json
ai-pkgs ws list
```

content.text: Now the workflows guide.

input.contents:
# Common workflows

End-to-end recipes for the most common ai-pkgs scenarios.

## 1. Bootstrap a project from `ai-package.json`

Clone a repo that already has `ai-package.json`, then restore every skill
and plugin into the local agent targets:

```bash
git clone owner/project
cd project
ai-pkgs install --yes
```

For CI:

```bash
ai-pkgs --ai install --yes
```

## 2. Add a skill from GitHub

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add vercel-labs/skills --skill tdd --agent cursor --project
```

After this, `ai-package.json` carries the entry and `.cursor/skills/tdd/`
contains the SKILL.md.

## 3. Add a plugin to multiple agents

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins add vercel/vercel-plugin --agent claude-code --agent cursor --yes
```

The persisted `targets: ["claude-code", "cursor"]` is what `ai-pkgs install`
uses to restore the plugin later.

## 4. Iterate on an installed skill

Move it into workspace mode, edit in place, push back to its remote:

```bash
ai-pkgs workspace link explain
# (edit .cursor/skills/explain/SKILL.md)
ai-pkgs workspace status
ai-pkgs workspace push explain -m "feat: improve examples"
```

## 5. Author a new skill from scratch and publish it

```bash
ai-pkgs skills add myteam/agent-skills --skill new-thing --workspace
# (edit .cursor/skills/new-thing/SKILL.md)
ai-pkgs workspace push new-thing -m "feat: introduce new-thing"
```

## 6. Resolve a workspace push conflict

```bash
ai-pkgs workspace push explain
# remote diverged; pick "Accept my changes" or "Resolve locally"

# Or in --ai mode, force-push:
ai-pkgs workspace push explain --accept-my-change --ai -m "chore: force"

# Or to merge manually:
# the CLI prints a /tmp/ai-pkgs-ws-XXXXX/ path; cd in, run
#   git pull --rebase origin <branch> && git push origin <branch>
# then run:
ai-pkgs workspace pull explain --force
```

## 7. Update Git-pinned skills and plugins

```bash
ai-pkgs skills outdated
ai-pkgs skills update --yes
ai-pkgs plugins outdated
ai-pkgs plugins update --yes
```

## 8. Remove a plugin and clean agent caches

```bash
ai-pkgs plugins remove vercel-plugin --uninstall --yes
```

## 9. Clear the Git cache after a network hiccup

```bash
ai-pkgs cache clear --provider github --source vercel-labs/skills
```

content.text:
All four tasks complete. Summary:

**1. JSDoc audit** — every workspace export and supporting helper now has either:
- `@example` showing input → output for pure functions (`formatWorkspaceList`, `formatWorkspaceListJson`, `parseWorkspaceSkillEntry`, `resolveCloneUrl`, `defaultCommitMessage`, `isPushRejected`, `computeNewVersion`, `classifyWorkspaceStatus`, `formatWorkspaceStatus`, `buildLinkEntry`, `discoverInstalledPaths`, `resolveRemoveTarget`, `emptyManifest`)
- `@example` with explicit `// Side effects:` notes for I/O commands (`runWorkspaceListCommand`, `runWorkspaceRemoveCommand`, `runWorkspaceLinkCommand`, `runWorkspacePushCommand`, `runWorkspacePullCommand`, `runWorkspaceStatusCommand`, `registerWorkspaceCommand`, `runWorkspaceCommand`)

**2. E2E tests** — new `apps/cli/tests/e2e/workspace.e2e.test.ts` with **10 tests** covering:
- `list` empty / `list --json` / `ws` alias
- `link --local` happy path and "not in skills" error
- `remove --yes` happy path / missing `--yes` in `--ai` fails / "not a workspace skill" error
- `status` reports `untracked` for the zero-SHA sentinel
- **`push`/`pull` round-trip** via `git insteadOf` redirecting a fake `gitlab:https://...` source to a local Git repo (`GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL` + `receive.denyCurrentBranch=updateInstead`). Verifies remote content changes, manifest version updates, then pulls a follow-up remote commit back.

**3. llms.txt refactor** — `apps/web/public/llms.txt` is now a clean reference-style index with markdown links. Twenty-eight per-topic files live under `apps/web/public/llms/`:
- `overview.txt`, `sources.txt`, `agent-targets.txt`, `cache.txt`, `install.txt`, `workflows.txt`
- `skills/{index,add,list,remove,outdated,update,vercel-migrate}.txt`
- `plugins/{index,init,add,list,targets,outdated,update,remove}.txt`
- `workspace/{index,link,remove,push,pull,status,list}.txt`

**4. Validation:** type-check pass, 155 unit tests pass, **36 e2e tests pass** (10 new workspace), biome clean, build success.
